title: Iterating Collections 🔄
description: Loop through any collection like a pro.

slides.title: Looping Through Collections 🔄
slides.content:
# Iterate Everything!

Python makes looping through any collection elegant:

**Lists/Tuples:**
```
for item in [1, 2, 3]:
    print(item)
```

**Dictionaries:**
```
for key, value in person.items():
    print(f"{key}: {value}")
```

**enumerate()** for index + value:
```
for i, item in enumerate(["a","b","c"]):
    print(f"{i}: {item}")
```

slides.title: Iteration Patterns 🎯

quizData.question: What does .items() return on a dictionary?
quizData.explanation: .items() returns all key-value pairs as tuples, perfect for looping.

quizData.question: What does zip() do?
quizData.explanation: zip() pairs corresponding elements from multiple iterables for parallel iteration.
